channel.title: Frontend on Plakar | The Open Standard for Backup and Restore
channel.description: Recent content in Frontend on Plakar | The Open Standard for Backup and Restore

item.title: I used to think frontend was easy
item.description: I’m a backend developer who ended up building a frontend. This series explains the Plakar UI stack — what’s in it, why it’s structured the way it is, and why it’s not as scary as the node_modules folder suggests.

item.title: Plakar UI #2: React
item.description: Why React exists and what problems it actually solves — explained to backend developers who know Go and are curious about how modern frontend UIs are built.

item.title: Plakar UI #3: TypeScript
item.description: Two TypeScript features that will surprise Go developers: incremental refactoring without blocking the compiler, and discriminated unions with automatic type narrowing.

item.title: The Plakar Frontend, Explained
item.description: A series for backend developers exploring the Plakar UI tech stack: pnpm, Turborepo, React, TypeScript, Zod, TanStack Query/Form/Table/Router, React Aria Components, Storybook, testing, and the frontend build pipeline.

item.title: Plakar UI #7: React Aria Components
item.description: Why we use React Aria Components as the foundation for our UI library — and why building accessible, keyboard-navigable widgets from scratch is much harder than it looks.

item.title: Plakar UI #10: Storybook
item.description: How we use Storybook to develop, document, and visually test our UI component library in isolation, independent of application logic.

item.title: Plakar UI #11: Testing Strategy
item.description: How we test the Plakar UI — unit tests for components, integration tests for user flows, and why we draw the line where we do.

item.title: Plakar UI #12: The Build Process
item.description: How we bundle and ship the Plakar UI — Vite, code splitting, environment configs, and what happens between writing code and serving it to users.

item.title: Plakar UI: Conclusion
item.description: Wrapping up the series — what we covered, the common thread running through every tool choice, and what comes next.
